summary: Extend `membership` to a typed reference — constrain the S₂ source set to artifacts also conforming to a named contract C, so a reference resolves to the right kind of thing.

per.section: The set scope (the roster) — predicates over a matched set

edit.description: Add an optional `source_contract: Option<RoleContract>` to `Membership`; extend `parse_membership` to read an optional `conforms_to` key (a template path or inline clauses, the same `RoleContract` a role's `contract` takes) and fold a malformed one into the existing `RoleBadMembership`. Absent ⇒ plain membership, unchanged.

In `out_of_set`, when `source_contract` is set, resolve it (`RoleContract::resolve`) and keep only S₂ candidates whose features pass `engine::validate` with no findings (i.e. conform) before drawing the allowed feature-set — reusing the same resolve+validate machinery `conformance` already runs. A non-resolving `conforms_to` is admissibility's to report, not `out_of_set`'s.

tests.asserts: A `[role.*]` `membership` carrying `conforms_to` draws its corpus-derived allowed set only from S₂ artifacts that conform to that contract: an S₁ filler whose field value matches only a non-conforming source is flagged; drop the `conforms_to` and the same value is in-set and silent.

A `membership` inline table with a `conforms_to` template-path (and with inline clauses) parses into a `Membership` carrying `Some(source_contract)`; a malformed `conforms_to` folds into `RoleBadMembership`.

acceptance: A `[role.*]` `membership` with a `conforms_to` constraint draws its corpus-derived allowed set only from source artifacts conforming to that contract — a filler whose field value comes solely from a non-conforming source is flagged; without the constraint that value is in-set and silent.
notes: Extends the shipped `membership` predicate (`compose.rs::Membership` + `roster.rs::out_of_set`); reuses `RoleContract::resolve` + `engine::validate` already in `roster.rs` (see `conformance`). Disjoint from parked SPEC-KIND-GATE (main.rs). Closes the fourth set-scope predicate; graph-scope degree/acyclic remain, awaiting an edge-extraction + graph foundation filed after this.

summary: Dispatch `check` over specs: embed `contracts/spec.toml`, project `ws.specs` via `spec_features`, and run admissibility + `engine::validate` (the advisory `max_lines` clause) over the spec features.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

gate.reason: the untracked `contracts/spec.toml` is curated human territory (build embeds it via `include_str!` but never writes/adds it); a human must commit it before the gate can embed it in a clean commit

edit.description: Add `BUILTIN_SPEC_CONTRACT = include_str!("../contracts/spec.toml")`; in the `Check` arm project `ws.specs` to spec features via `extract::spec_features`, parse the spec contract, and extend the diagnostic stream with `engine::admissibility` + `engine::validate` over them.

tests.asserts: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — the over-length specs trip `max_lines` at advisory severity and the run still exits 0 absent `--deny-advisories`.

acceptance: `temper check` over an imported corpus runs the spec contract — flagging the over-length specs at advisory severity and exiting 0 (non-blocking) absent `--deny-advisories`.
notes: Build-order: needs SPEC-FEATURES (the extractor, shipped — `extract::spec_features` on disk) plus a human to commit the untracked `contracts/spec.toml`. Runs only spec.toml's active `max_lines` clause; its two commented clauses (decisions-name-alternatives, references-resolve) await `(decision-marker-predicate)` and the references-resolve frontier — both also downstream of that spec.toml commit.
